claim: Thirteenth MCC measurement: Tier1XgbDetector trained on four feature slices (esm2_only 1280-dim, priors_only 3-dim, esm2_plus_priors 1283-dim, stacked 1299-dim) with 5-fold stratified CV at split_seed=42. HONEST NEGATIVE RESULT: no XGBoost variant beats the v10b priors-union rule baseline on either the full 455-gene set (aggregated MCC: esm2_only 0.241, priors_only 0.211, esm2_plus_priors 0.197, stacked 0.217 vs v10b-rule 0.364) or the balanced-40 panel (esm2_only 0.402, priors_only 0.000, esm2_plus_priors 0.603, stacked 0.551 vs v10b-rule 0.800). Only ESM-2 embeddings are populated; AlphaFold-DB and MACE-OFF parquets are empty because Syn3A's synthetic taxonomy (taxid 2144189) is absent from UniProt and no curated SMILES map exists yet. This measurement closes Session-15 item 0c (Tier-1 XGBoost baseline) with a falsification: learned features on ESM-2 alone do not lift MCC on this 5.3:1-imbalanced benchmark; the remaining MCC gap lives in the simulator-biology incompleteness (iMB155 pathways + ribosome assembly), not in the detector family.
source_detail: Reproducible via 'python scripts/run_tier1_sweep.py --cache-dir cell_sim/features/cache --trajectory-csv outputs/predictions_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_composed_all455_v10a.csv --panel-seed 42 --n-panel 40 --cv-split-seed 42 --n-splits 5 --out outputs/tier1_sweep_v11.json'. Elapsed 15.0s on the 4-core reference box. Full JSON preserved at outputs/tier1_sweep_v11.json.

cache_availability.esm2_650M: populated; 455 rows x 1280 dims; zero NaN; sha 0d572640...
cache_availability.alphafold_db: empty parquet (0 rows x 9 dims); Syn3A taxid 2144189 not in UniProt
cache_availability.mace_off_kcat: empty parquet (0 rows x 7 dims); no curated SMILES map for SBML species

caveats: Honest negative result. The v10b rule baseline (MCC 0.364 full / 0.800 balanced) remains unbeaten by any Tier-1 XGBoost slice. The new ML path did NOT produce a lift.

Why ML fails here (likely): (a) class imbalance 5.3:1 on the full set; XGBoost's default calibration biases toward the majority positive class, inflating TP at the cost of catastrophic FP growth (47-48 FP vs v10b's 6 FP). (b) Only 455 labeled rows for 1280 ESM-2 features -> tree models overfit despite reg_alpha/reg_lambda. (c) The priors the rule stack uses already cover the easy signal; the remaining loci are precisely the ones ESM-2 alone can't disambiguate.

priors_only XGBoost collapses to MCC 0.000 on the balanced-40 panel because the 3-dim feature space puts 8 positives and 8 negatives in the identical (0,0,0) cell; XGBoost trains on 32 rows per fold which is too few to recover any split. The v10b rule 'union' decision on the same features scores 0.800 because it is union-over-priors, not a learned threshold.

AlphaFold-DB and MACE-OFF parquets are empty by design until Session-15 items 0a (UniProt mapping for taxid 2144189) and 0b (BiGG -> SMILES map) land. Populating either should push 'stacked' above esm2_plus_priors; populating both might finally cross the v10b floor.
The test suite covers the detector end-to-end (13 new tests, 185 total passing). The CV smoke test skips gracefully if xgboost or scikit-learn aren't installed.

Implication for roadmap: the honest next lever is simulator-biology (Session-15 items 1 ribosome complex + iMB155 patches), NOT more detector engineering. The Tier-1 feature path stays worth completing (AlphaFold + MACE populate) because structural + kinetic features address the 15 translation-machinery FNs that ESM-2 embeddings alone cannot.
